data.text: I'll start by reading the key files mentioned in the bead description before making any changes.
Let me check the test files to see what exists and also understand the current state better.

data.output:
1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"regexp"
10		"strings"
11		"sync"
12		"time"
13	
14		agenttry "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/try"
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/work"
16		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
17		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
18		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
19		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
20	)
21	
22	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
23	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
24	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
25	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
26	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
27		Log             io.Writer
28		CleanupLog      io.Writer
29		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
30		CleanupInterval time.Duration
31		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
32		EventSink       io.Writer
33		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
34		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
35		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
36		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
37		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
38		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
39		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
40		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
41		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
42		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
43		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
44		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
45		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
46		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
47		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
48		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
49		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
50		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
51		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
52		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
53		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
54		Once           bool
55		PollInterval   time.Duration
56		NoReview       bool
57		LabelFilter    string
58		SessionID      string
59		WorkerID       string
60		ProjectRoot    string
61		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
62		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
63		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
64		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
65		TargetBeadID string
66		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
67		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
68		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid a poll-interval-sized delay
69		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
70		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
71		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
72		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
73	}
74	
75	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
76	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
77	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
78	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
79	
80	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
81	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
82	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
83	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
84	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
85	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
86	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
87	
88	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
89	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
90	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
91	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
92	// advance the base branch.
93	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
94	
95	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
96	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
97	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
98	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
99	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
100	// worker.

data.text: There's already a test file for pre-claim serial tests. Let me read it and the other test files to understand what's already implemented.

data.output:
1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"context"
6		"encoding/json"
7		"fmt"
8		"strings"
9		"sync"
10		"sync/atomic"
11		"testing"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
16		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
17		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
18	)
19	
20	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreClaimIntakeRunsOncePerBead is AC #1:
21	// With two concurrent workers on one ready bead, only the worker that wins
22	// Store.Claim invokes the model-backed PreClaimIntakeHook. The loser loses at
23	// Claim (picker.claim_race), never reaches intake, and moves on.
24	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreClaimIntakeRunsOncePerBead(t *testing.T) {
25		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
26		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
27		only := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-concurrent-intake", Title: "concurrent intake", Priority: 0}
28		require.NoError(t, store.Create(only))
29	
30		var intakeCalls atomic.Int32
31	
32		// Intake hook blocks so that Worker B has time to lose the Claim race
33		// before Worker A's intake returns.
34		intakeStarted := make(chan struct{}, 1)
35		intakeRelease := make(chan struct{})
36	
37		makeRuntime := func(assignee string) (config.ResolvedConfig, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) {
38			opts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: assignee}
39			rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(opts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(opts))
40			rt := ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
41				Once: true,
42				PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
43					intakeCalls.Add(1)
44					select {
45					case intakeStarted <- struct{}{}:
46					default:
47					}
48					select {
49					case <-intakeRelease:
50					case <-ctx.Done():
51					}
52					return PreClaimIntakeResult{Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic}, nil
53				},
54			}
55			return rcfg, rt
56		}
57	
58		executor := ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
59			return ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID, Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ResultRev: "rev"}, nil
60		})
61		workerA := &ExecuteBeadWorker{Store: store, Executor: executor}
62		workerB := &ExecuteBeadWorker{Store: store, Executor: executor}
63	
64		var wg sync.WaitGroup
65		wg.Add(2)
66		go func() {
67			defer wg.Done()
68			rcfg, rt := makeRuntime("worker-a")
69			_, _ = workerA.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, rt)
70		}()
71		go func() {
72			defer wg.Done()
73			rcfg, rt := makeRuntime("worker-b")
74			_, _ = workerB.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, rt)
75		}()
76	
77		// Wait for exactly one intake invocation to start, then release it.
78		// If neither starts within 5s the test hangs on wg.Wait and fails via
79		// t.Cleanup timeout rather than silently passing.
80		select {
81		case <-intakeStarted:
82		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
83			t.Fatal("intake hook never started — no worker claimed the bead")
84		}
85		close(intakeRelease)
86		wg.Wait()
87	
88		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), intakeCalls.Load(),
89			"only the claiming worker must enter intake; the Claim-loser must not run the model-backed hook")
90	}
91	
92	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreDispatchLintDoesNotDuplicateEvents is AC #2:
93	// Two concurrent workers on the same bead must not append duplicate
94	// bead-quality.lint events. With claim-first ordering, only the owning worker
95	// appends the lint event; the loser never reaches the lint gate.
96	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreDispatchLintDoesNotDuplicateEvents(t *testing.T) {
97		inner := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
98		require.NoError(t, inner.Init())
99		only := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-concurrent-lint", Title: "concurrent lint", Priority: 0}
100		require.NoError(t, inner.Create(only))
101	
102		// Lint hook blocks to widen the concurrency window.
103		lintStarted := make(chan struct{}, 1)
104		lintRelease := make(chan struct{})
105	
106		makeRuntime := func(assignee string) (config.ResolvedConfig, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) {
107			opts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: assignee}
108			rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(opts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(opts))
109			rt := ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
110				Once: true,
111				PreDispatchLintHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error) {
112					select {
113					case lintStarted <- struct{}{}:
114					default:
115					}
116					select {
117					case <-lintRelease:
118					case <-ctx.Done():
119					}
120					return LintResult{Score: 9, Rationale: "ready"}, nil
121				},
122			}
123			return rcfg, rt
124		}
125	
126		executor := ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
127			return ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID, Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ResultRev: "rev"}, nil
128		})
129		workerA := &ExecuteBeadWorker{Store: inner, Executor: executor}
130		workerB := &ExecuteBeadWorker{Store: inner, Executor: executor}
131	
132		var wg sync.WaitGroup
133		wg.Add(2)
134		go func() {
135			defer wg.Done()
136			rcfg, rt := makeRuntime("worker-a")
137			_, _ = workerA.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, rt)
138		}()
139		go func() {
140			defer wg.Done()
141			rcfg, rt := makeRuntime("worker-b")
142			_, _ = workerB.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, rt)
143		}()
144	
145		select {
146		case <-lintStarted:
147		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
148			t.Fatal("lint hook never started")
149		}
150		close(lintRelease)
151		wg.Wait()
152	
153		events, err := inner.Events(only.ID)
154		require.NoError(t, err)
155		var lintEvents []bead.BeadEvent
156		for _, ev := range events {
157			if ev.Kind == "bead-quality.lint" {
158				lintEvents = append(lintEvents, ev)
159			}
160		}
161		assert.Equal(t, 1, len(lintEvents),
162			"exactly one bead-quality.lint event must be appended; concurrent workers must not duplicate it")
163	}
164	
165	// TestPreClaimIntakeRewriteRequiresOwnedReservation is AC #3:
166	// With actionable_but_rewritten intake, only the claiming worker applies
167	// description/acceptance rewrites. Concurrent losers cannot double-rewrite
168	// or append conflicting events because they never reach applyPreClaimIntakeRewrite.
169	func TestPreClaimIntakeRewriteRequiresOwnedReservation(t *testing.T) {
170		inner := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
171		require.NoError(t, inner.Init())
172	
173		original := &bead.Bead{
174			ID:          "ddx-rewrite-concurrent",
175			Title:       "rewrite ownership",
176			Priority:    0,
177			Description: "PROBLEM\noriginal problem\n\nROOT CAUSE\noriginal cause\n\nPROPOSED FIX\noriginal fix\n",
178			Acceptance:  "1. original criterion\n2. name the test",
179		}
180		require.NoError(t, inner.Create(original))
181	
182		var rewriteCalls atomic.Int32
183		rewriteStarted := make(chan struct{}, 1)
184		rewriteRelease := make(chan struct{})
185	
186		makeRuntime := func(assignee string) (config.ResolvedConfig, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) {
187			opts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: assignee}
188			rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(opts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(opts))
189			rt := ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
190				Once: true,
191				PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
192					rewriteCalls.Add(1)
193					select {
194					case rewriteStarted <- struct{}{}:
195					default:
196					}
197					select {
198					case <-rewriteRelease:
199					case <-ctx.Done():
200					}
201					return PreClaimIntakeResult{
202						Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten,
203						Detail:  "safe refinement",
204						Rewrite: PreClaimIntakeRewrite{
205							Description:   "PROBLEM\noriginal problem\n\nROOT CAUSE\noriginal cause\n\nPROPOSED FIX\noriginal fix\n\nAdd explicit validation step.",
206							Acceptance:    "1. original criterion\n2. name the test\n3. lefthook run pre-commit passes",
207							ChangedFields: []string{"description", "acceptance"},
208						},
209					}, nil
210				},
211			}
212			return rcfg, rt
213		}
214	
215		executor := ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
216			return ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID, Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ResultRev: "rev"}, nil
217		})
218		workerA := &ExecuteBeadWorker{Store: inner, Executor: executor}
219		workerB := &ExecuteBeadWorker{Store: inner, Executor: executor}
220	
221		var wg sync.WaitGroup
222		wg.Add(2)
223		go func() {
224			defer wg.Done()
225			rcfg, rt := makeRuntime("worker-a")
226			_, _ = workerA.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, rt)
227		}()
228		go func() {
229			defer wg.Done()
230			rcfg, rt := makeRuntime("worker-b")
231			_, _ = workerB.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, rt)
232		}()
233	
234		select {
235		case <-rewriteStarted:
236		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
237			t.Fatal("intake hook never started")
238		}
239		close(rewriteRelease)
240		wg.Wait()
241	
242		// Only the claiming worker runs intake and reaches the rewrite path.
243		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), rewriteCalls.Load(),
244			"only the claiming worker must run the rewrite intake hook; concurrent losers must not reach it")
245	
246		// The description must be rewritten exactly once: contains the added
247		// text but does not contain it twice (no double-rewrite).
248		got, err := inner.Get(original.ID)
249		require.NoError(t, err)
250		count := strings.Count(got.Description, "Add explicit validation step.")
251		assert.Equal(t, 1, count,
252			"description must be rewritten exactly once; double-rewrite would indicate a concurrent write")
253	
254		// Exactly one intake-rewritten event must exist.
255		events, err := inner.Events(original.ID)
256		require.NoError(t, err)
257		var rewriteEvents int
258		for _, ev := range events {
259			if ev.Kind == "intake-rewritten" {
260				rewriteEvents++
261			}
262		}
263		assert.Equal(t, 1, rewriteEvents,
264			"exactly one intake-rewritten event must be recorded; duplicates indicate concurrent rewrite")
265	}
266	
267	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerReadinessRejectReleasesOrParksClaim is AC #4:
268	// Covers the post-claim outcomes when intake or lint rejects the bead:
269	//   - infra error: fail-open, proceed to execution
270	//   - ambiguous_needs_human: park bead (needs_human label) and unclaim
271	//   - lint-blocked: unclaim bead, no execution
272	//   - actionable_atomic: proceed to implementation normally
273	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerReadinessRejectReleasesOrParksClaim(t *testing.T) {
274		t.Run("intake_infra_error_proceeds_to_execution", func(t *testing.T) {
275			store, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
276			var execCalled atomic.Int32
277			worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
278				Store: store,
279				Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
280					execCalled.Add(1)
281					return ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID, Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ResultRev: "rev"}, nil
282				}),
283			}
284			opts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
285			rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(opts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(opts))
286	
287			result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
288				Once: true,
289				PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
290					return PreClaimIntakeResult{}, fmt.Errorf("intake service timeout")
291				},
292			})
293			require.NoError(t, err)
294			require.NotNil(t, result)
295	
296			assert.Equal(t, int32(1), execCalled.Load(), "infra error must fail open and proceed to execution")
297			assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
298	
299			got, err := store.Get(candidate.ID)
300			require.NoError(t, err)
301			assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status, "bead must be closed after successful execution")
302		})
303	
304		t.Run("ambiguous_needs_human_parks_and_unclaims", func(t *testing.T) {
305			store, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
306			var eventSink bytes.Buffer
307			worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
308				Store: store,
309				Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
310					t.Fatal("executor must not run for terminal intake outcomes")
311					return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
312				}),
313			}
314			opts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
315			rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(opts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(opts))
316	
317			result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
318				Once:      true,
319				EventSink: &eventSink,
320				PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
321					return PreClaimIntakeResult{
322						Outcome: PreClaimIntakeAmbiguousNeedsHuman,
323						Detail:  "missing root cause with file:line",
324					}, nil
325				},
326			})
327			require.NoError(t, err)
328			require.NotNil(t, result)
329	
330			assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts, "terminal intake outcome must not count as an attempt")
331	
332			// Bead must be back to open (unclaimed) with needs_human label.
333			got, err := store.Get(candidate.ID)
334			require.NoError(t, err)
335			assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status, "bead must be unclaimed after terminal intake")
336			assert.Contains(t, got.Labels, bead.LabelNeedsHuman,
337				"terminal intake outcome must park bead with needs_human label")
338	
339			// An intake.blocked event must be recorded on the bead.
340			events, err := store.Events(candidate.ID)
341			require.NoError(t, err)
342			var foundBlocked bool
343			for _, ev := range events {
344				if ev.Kind != "intake.blocked" {
345					continue
346				}
347				foundBlocked = true
348				var body map[string]any
349				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &body))
350				assert.Equal(t, string(PreClaimIntakeAmbiguousNeedsHuman), body["intake_outcome"])
351			}
352			assert.True(t, foundBlocked, "an intake.blocked event must be recorded when bead is parked")
353	
354			// The event stream must contain pre_claim_intake.blocked.
355			assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "pre_claim_intake.blocked")
356		})
357	
358		t.Run("lint_blocked_unclaims_without_execution", func(t *testing.T) {
359			store, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
360			var eventSink bytes.Buffer
361			worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
362				Store: store,
363				Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
364					t.Fatal("executor must not run when lint blocks dispatch")
365					return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
366				}),
367			}
368	
369			opts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
370				Assignee:                           "worker",
371				BeadQualityLintBlockThresholdScore: 7,
372			}
373			rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(opts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(opts))
374	
375			result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
376				Once:      true,
377				EventSink: &eventSink,
378				PreDispatchLintHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error) {
379					return LintResult{Score: 4, Rationale: "incomplete AC"}, nil
380				},
381			})
382			require.NoError(t, err)
383			require.NotNil(t, result)
384	
385			assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts, "lint-blocked must not count as an attempt")
386	
387			// Bead must be back to open (unclaimed).
388			got, err := store.Get(candidate.ID)
389			require.NoError(t, err)
390			assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, got.Status, "bead must be unclaimed after lint block")
391	
392			assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "pre_dispatch_lint.blocked")
393		})
394	
395		t.Run("actionable_atomic_proceeds_to_implementation", func(t *testing.T) {
396			store, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
397			var execCalled atomic.Int32
398			worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
399				Store: store,
400				Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
401					execCalled.Add(1)
402					return ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID, Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ResultRev: "rev"}, nil
403				}),
404			}
405			opts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
406			rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(opts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(opts))
407	
408			result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
409				Once: true,
410				PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
411					return PreClaimIntakeResult{Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic}, nil
412				},
413			})
414			require.NoError(t, err)
415			require.NotNil(t, result)
416	
417			assert.Equal(t, int32(1), execCalled.Load(), "actionable_atomic must proceed to implementation")
418			assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
419	
420			got, err := store.Get(candidate.ID)
421			require.NoError(t, err)
422			assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)
423		})
424	}
425
